commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: docs(install): correct Kynver patch channel guide for seam-only reality
target.body:
## Summary

Corrects the Kynver patch channel install guide so it matches the seam-only
reality of the fork patch line, and brings in the cross-linked deploy-pipeline
reference it depends on. This addresses three review blockers on the prior
artifact:

1. **Seam-only Kynver wording.** The lead paragraph, page summary, and audience
   bullet no longer claim first-class Kynver tools ship today. They describe the
   public *agent-harness tool seam* for building Kynver tools. The dedicated
   "Kynver first-class tools" section states explicitly that the seam ships in
   the dogfood line while the `@kynver-app/openclaw-agent-os` plugin is a
   not-guaranteed follow-up.
2. **Internal-name cleanup.** No internal operator names appear in the public
   doc.
3. **phase1 branch prefix.** The phase-1 responsiveness branch is referenced as
   `phase1-openclaw-responsiveness` (no `agent/` prefix), matching the real fork
   branch name. The other `agent/`-prefixed branches are distinct, correct
   branches and keep their prefix.

Adds two net-new docs pages (no edits to existing files):

- `docs/install/kynver-patch-channel.md`
- `docs/reference/deploy-pipeline.md`

## Verification

Behavior addressed: corrected Kynver patch-channel install guide (seam-only
wording, internal-name removal, phase1 branch prefix) plus its cross-linked
deploy-pipeline reference.
Real environment tested: linked git worktree off `origin/main` (bc9e601),
`node_modules` not installed; verification done with git/text tooling that needs
no dependency install.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- git diff --check origin/main HEAD
- diffed applied files byte-for-byte against the reviewed source commit
- resolved every cross-doc route link to a real docs file on this branch
- resolved every intra-page anchor to a real heading slug
Evidence after fix:
- git diff --check: no whitespace or conflict-marker errors
- applied content is byte-identical to the reviewed source commit (empty diff)
- all 7 cross-doc links resolve (`/gateway/doctor`, `/gateway/troubleshooting`,
  `/install`, `/install/development-channels`, `/install/updating`,
  `/reference/deploy-pipeline`, `/install/kynver-patch-channel`)
- all 4 intra-page anchors resolve (`#what-is-not-guaranteed-yet`,
  `#kynver-first-class-tools`, `#agent-harness-operator-workflow`,
  `#follow-ups`)
- diff scope is exactly the two new files, no other paths touched
Observed result after fix: docs are link-clean and content-correct; the three
blockers are confirmed fixed.
What was not tested: full Mintlify build, markdownlint-cli2, and MDX compile were
not run because they require a `pnpm install` not available in this linked
worktree. The two new pages are not registered in `docs/docs.json` navigation
(this matches the reviewed scope, which did not modify nav); a maintainer should
decide whether they belong in the sidebar.
target.author: totalsolutionsync

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
